MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fifth Cause Of Action Against Cross-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation
Off Calendar Oct-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fifth Cause Of Action Against (declaratory relief-duty to Defend) Against Cross-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation.

Cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication on its fifth cause of action for declaratory relief for a duty to defend against cross-defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America is granted. Based on the undisputed facts, the City is entitled to a declaration that Thyssenkrupp is obligated to defend it against the claims alleged by plaintiff Maria Calderon in this case. Per the duty to defend language in the parties' contract and Crawford Weather Shield Manufacturing, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal. 44 541, Thyssenkrupp became obligated to defend the City against the claims alleged by Ms. Calderon in this case once the City made a demand that it be defended. All of Thyssenkrupp's procedural arguments lack merit. Thyssenkrupp's argument that it is not obligated to defend the City until there is a determination that it acted negligently is contrary to both the language of the parties' agreement and Crawford. Thyssenkrupp's argument that Crawford only applies to pre-2006 residential construction contracts is belied by Crawford itself. Crawford makes clear that it is construing duty to defend language in a noninsurance context which does not condition the duty to defend on a finding of negligence or other wrongful conduct. Consequently, Crawford is on point authority for construing the duty to defend language that is in the parties' contract.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
